quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Photovoltaic cell"?

options.text: Semiconductor device converting sunlight directly into electricity, composed of silicon or other semiconductors
The largest harvest possible indefinitely without reducing stock below level supporting maximum reproduction
Scarcity of available water relative to demand, measured as annual freshwater withdrawal relative to renewable freshwater supply
Ratio of water used productively to total water supplied, improved through drip irrigation and deficit irrigation techniques

quiz_questions.explanation: "Photovoltaic cell" is defined as: Semiconductor device converting sunlight directly into electricity, composed of silicon or other semiconductors. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Wind turbine"?

options.text: Device converting kinetic energy from wind into electricity, with capacity scaling with height and rotor diameter
The finite rate of increase; λ = 1 equals stable population, λ > 1 equals growth, λ < 1 equals decline
The distribution of a population among different age classes, often displayed as a population pyramid
Protection of species in their natural habitat and ecosystems, preferred over ex situ conservation where possible

quiz_questions.explanation: "Wind turbine" is defined as: Device converting kinetic energy from wind into electricity, with capacity scaling with height and rotor diameter. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Hydroelectric power"?

options.text: Reproductive failure where eggshells become fragile and break during incubation, caused by DDE (DDT metabolite) disrupting calcium metabolism
Species whose impact on ecosystems is disproportionate to their abundance; removal causes ecological collapse
Splitting of heavy atomic nuclei (uranium-235, plutonium-239) releasing enormous energy, the basis of nuclear power
Generation of electricity from falling water controlled by dams, providing both energy storage and flood control benefits

quiz_questions.explanation: "Hydroelectric power" is defined as: Generation of electricity from falling water controlled by dams, providing both energy storage and flood control benefits. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Capacity factor"?

options.text: The largest harvest possible indefinitely without reducing stock below level supporting maximum reproduction
A system where source populations produce surplus individuals dispersing to sink populations unable to maintain themselves
Ratio of actual energy output to maximum possible output, varies from 15% (solar) to 90% (nuclear) depending on technology
Active process of intentionally restoring degraded ecosystems to a more natural or desired state

quiz_questions.explanation: "Capacity factor" is defined as: Ratio of actual energy output to maximum possible output, varies from 15% (solar) to 90% (nuclear) depending on technology. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Grid integration"?

options.text: Harvesting fish faster than populations can reproduce, exceeding sustainable yield and causing population decline
The complete sequence of successional stages from pioneer community through intermediate stages to climax community in a given area
Atmospheric gas (CO₂, CH₄, N₂O, fluorine gases) absorbing infrared radiation and warming earth's surface
Connecting dispersed renewable generation to electrical grids, managing variability through storage and demand management

quiz_questions.explanation: "Grid integration" is defined as: Connecting dispersed renewable generation to electrical grids, managing variability through storage and demand management. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "Semiconductor device converting sunlight directly into electricity, composed of silicon or other semiconductors"?

options.text: Photovoltaic cell
Facilitation
Peak oil
Stockholm Convention

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Photovoltaic cell".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "Device converting kinetic energy from wind into electricity, with capacity scaling with height and rotor diameter"?

options.text: Halons
Wind turbine
Atmosphere
Erosion

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Wind turbine".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "Generation of electricity from falling water controlled by dams, providing both energy storage and flood control benefits"?

options.text: Convection
Hydroelectric power
Hydrological cycle
Doubling time

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Hydroelectric power".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "Ratio of actual energy output to maximum possible output, varies from 15% (solar) to 90% (nuclear) depending on technology"?

options.text: Secondary pollutant
Capacity factor
Inbreeding depression
Bioavailability

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Capacity factor".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "Connecting dispersed renewable generation to electrical grids, managing variability through storage and demand management"?

options.text: Grid integration
Total fertility rate (TFR)
Mass extinction
Methane

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Grid integration".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following statements about Renewable Energy Sources is accurate?

options.text: Climate impacts include temperature extremes, precipitation changes, sea-level rise, and ecosystem disruptions affecting food, water, and health security.

Renewable Energy Sources including solar, wind, hydroelectric, geothermal, and biomass are replenished on human timescales and produce zero or low lifecycle greenhouse gas emissions compared to fossil fuels.
Understanding agricultural impacts is essential for feeding growing populations while preserving ecosystem integrity.
Understanding water system dynamics is essential for managing freshwater scarcity and protecting aquatic ecosystems.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Renewable Energy Sources as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which statement correctly describes an aspect of Renewable Energy Sources?

options.text: Renewable energy capacity is growing rapidly globally but faces challenges of intermittency (solar, wind), geographic limitations, and grid integration requirements.

Bioaccumulation and Biomagnification describe the accumulation of persistent toxins in organism tissues over time and their increase in concentration across trophic levels, with top predators accumulating to levels 10,000–1,000,000 times water...

Conservation of Biodiversity employs science-based strategies to prevent extinction and preserve ecosystem function, including protected areas, habitat restoration, captive breeding, and sustainable resource management.
Coal, Oil, and Natural Gas represent distinct fossil fuel types with different formation, extraction, and combustion characteristics.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Renewable Energy Sources as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which is true specifically about Renewable Energy Sources?

options.text: Indoor Air Quality impacts human health through exposure to particulates, volatile organic compounds, carbon monoxide, radon, biological pollutants (mold, allergens), and secondhand smoke in poorly ventilated spaces.
Disruptions to nutrient cycles through industrial processes, agriculture, and combustion have fundamentally altered planetary biogeochemistry and created significant environmental challenges.

Ecosystem Services and Biomes provide tangible and intangible benefits to humans, including provisioning services (food, water, materials), regulating services (climate regulation, disease control), supporting services (nutrient cycling,...
A renewable energy transition is essential for meeting climate targets but requires investment in generation capacity, storage, and grid modernization.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Renewable Energy Sources as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which best explains a key idea of Renewable Energy Sources?

options.text: Persistent organic pollutants (POPs)—including DDT, PCBs, dioxins—resist degradation and bioaccumulate through food web transfer, devastating top predators (eagles, falcons) through eggshell thinning and reproductive failure.

Resource depletion reflects overconsumption in wealthy nations (approximately 2.7 global hectares per person on average, but ~8 gha in the United States and other high-consumption nations) and accelerating extraction in developing countries.

Renewable Energy Sources including solar, wind, hydroelectric, geothermal, and biomass are replenished on human timescales and produce zero or low lifecycle greenhouse gas emissions compared to fossil fuels.
Loss of genetic diversity through habitat fragmentation, population bottlenecks, and inbreeding weakens species' long-term survival prospects and reduces the stability of entire ecosystems.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Renewable Energy Sources as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which accurately reflects the content on Renewable Energy Sources?

options.text: Bioaccumulation and Biomagnification describe the accumulation of persistent toxins in organism tissues over time and their increase in concentration across trophic levels, with top predators accumulating to levels 10,000–1,000,000 times water...
Renewable energy capacity is growing rapidly globally but faces challenges of intermittency (solar, wind), geographic limitations, and grid integration requirements.
Age structure shapes future population changes independent of current birth and death rates, making it crucial for long-term planning.
Understanding water system dynamics is essential for managing freshwater scarcity and protecting aquatic ecosystems.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Renewable Energy Sources as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following is a real-world application of Renewable Energy Sources?

options.text: Managing atmospheric particulates through emission controls and clean air standards to improve visibility and reduce respiratory health impacts
Tracking endangered sea turtle populations using tagging studies to determine migration routes and identify critical habitat protection areas
Siting large wind farms to maximize resource utilization while minimizing impacts on bird migration and local landscapes
Managing copper mining impacts in Chile's Atacama Desert through water treatment systems addressing acid mine drainage and water depletion

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Renewable Energy Sources.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which example demonstrates the practical relevance of Renewable Energy Sources?

options.text: Monitoring freshwater aquifer depletion rates (Ogallala, Arabian, North China Plain) to prevent permanent loss of irrigation capacity
Installing distributed solar arrays on rooftops and in parking structures to generate electricity closer to demand centers
Using PVA models for Florida panther recovery to determine required population growth rates and habitat connectivity for species recovery
Monitoring wolf population recovery in Yellowstone using capture-recapture methods to estimate population size and assess reintroduction success

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Renewable Energy Sources.

quiz_questions.question_text: How is Renewable Energy Sources applied in real-world contexts?

options.text: Tracking endangered sea turtle populations using tagging studies to determine migration routes and identify critical habitat protection areas
Managing seasonal hydroelectric variability through water storage, coordinated operation with other generation sources, and demand management
Managing harmful algal blooms (cyanobacteria) in freshwater lakes through nutrient reduction and copper sulfate applications in emergency situations
Treating municipal wastewater through primary (settling), secondary (biological), and tertiary (nutrient removal) steps to protect receiving waters

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Renewable Energy Sources.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which scenario illustrates the principles of Renewable Energy Sources?

options.text: Limiting UV exposure through tanning ban regulation and sun safety education as ozone layer recovery proceeds
Siting large wind farms to maximize resource utilization while minimizing impacts on bird migration and local landscapes
Modeling sea-level rise impacts on coastal cities and small island nations, informing relocation planning and adaptation infrastructure investment
Establishing payment for ecosystem services programs paying forest-dependent communities to maintain standing forests instead of clearing

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Renewable Energy Sources.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which best shows Renewable Energy Sources in practice?

options.text: Retrofitting existing buildings with insulation, efficient HVAC, and LED lighting to reduce energy consumption by 30–50%
Projecting Japan's shrinking population using an inverted age pyramid to anticipate workforce shortages and retirement system challenges
Installing distributed solar arrays on rooftops and in parking structures to generate electricity closer to demand centers
Using spotted owls as umbrella species in Pacific Northwest forest conservation to protect old-growth forest ecosystems benefiting hundreds of species

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Renewable Energy Sources.
